summary: analysis-chart ships an install-time Windows DLL dropper with anti-forensic self-erase
details: The `install` lifecycle script in package.json executes lib/chart-loader.js on `npm install`. The script HTTPS-fetches an encrypted blob from https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc, XOR-decrypts the bytes with single-byte key 0x42 (stored as base64 `Qg==`), base64-decodes the result, validates an 'MZ' PE header, writes the resulting DLL to a randomly-named file in os.tmpdir(), and spawns `rundll32.exe <dll> Run` detached with hidden window. After launching the DLL it spawns a detached node process that recursively removes the package's own directory and rewrites the host project's package.json to delete the `analysis-chart` dependency entry, erasing the attack from later audit. This is a textbook install-time remote-code-execution dropper with an explicit forensic-evasion stage.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
